items.headline: Astros star Carlos Correa faces season-ending surgery on a torn tendon in his ankle
items.description:
HOUSTON (AP) — Houston’s Carlos Correa has a torn tendon in his left ankle that will require season-ending surgery, the star infielder said Wednesday.
Correa was injured Tuesday while taking swings in the batting cage before a game against the Los Angeles Dodgers.

items.headline: Browns owners Dee and Jimmy Haslam donate $12.5 million toward blood cancer research and treatment
items.description:
CLEVELAND (AP) — Browns owners Dee and Jimmy Haslam have donated $12.5 million toward blood cancer research and treatment.
The donation includes $10 million to the Oxford-Harrington Rare Disease Centre in England for research and drug development focused on chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) and other rare blood cancers. The other $2.5 million will go to Cleveland's University Hospitals Seidman Cancer Center, which will establish an endowed chair in CLL research and an innovation fund toward driving advancements in care.

items.headline: This Date in Baseball - Fred McGriff sets MLB record by homering in his 34th major league ballpark
items.description:
May 7
1917 — Babe Ruth of the Red Sox allowed two hits as he outdueled Walter Johnson of the Washington Senators 1-0. Ruth knocked in the winning run with a sacrifice fly.

items.headline: Coaches group supports earlier CFP finish and proposes changes to accomplish that
items.description:
WACO, Texas (AP) — The American Football Coaches Association is proposing the elimination of conference championship games and other changes as part of its non-binding recommendation for the College Football Playoff to be completed by the second Monday each January.
While the AFCA has no authority within the NCAA or CFP governance structures, FBS coaches are part of the group's board and membership. The AFCA publicly revealed its positions Tuesday, after they were discussed and adopted by board members at an annual meeting last week.

items.headline: Stefon Diggs' acquittal clears path for return to the field but he could still face NFL discipline
items.description:
Stefon Diggs’ acquittal in court clears a path for the four-time Pro Bowl wide receiver to return to the field.
He still could face discipline from the NFL.

items.headline: Utah Mammoth ready to take next big step after franchise's first playoff run
items.description:
When general manager Bill Armstrong realized adding pieces here and there wasn't working for the Arizona Coyotes, he blew up most of the roster.
Armstrong kept the core young players to serve as the foundation for the future and amassed a stockpile of draft picks, hoping to rebuild a franchise that had been mired in mediocrity.
